name: The Water Route along the Reatillo River
name_original: La Ruta del Agua por el Río Reatillo
description: Hike the easy 2.6-mile Water Route along the Reatillo River, featuring natural pools like Charco Gruñidor and Mediterranean forests.
region_intro: Hiking around Sot De Chera offers access to a diverse natural landscape within the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural and Geological Park. The terrain features deep gorges, mountains reaching up to 1,100 meters, and fertile valleys. The area is characterized by significant geological formations, including travertines and impressive arch-shaped folds. The River Sot traverses the region, creating natural pools and riparian forests. Best hiking trails around Sot De Chera The most popular hiking route is Ruta de los Pantaneros and Hanging Bridges Trail, a 12.9 miles (20.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 46 minutes to complete. This difficult route features demanding terrain and incorporates hanging bridges. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador del Morroncillo — Circular from Sot de Chera (PR-CV 223), a moderate 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This circular route leads to the Mirador del Morroncillo viewpoint, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Buseo Reservoir and Barranco de la Hoz Loop, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through the Buseo Reservoir area and the Barranco de la Hoz, often completed in about 4 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Sot De Chera is defined by deep gorges, towering mountains, and the presence of the Buseo Reservoir and the Reatillo River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging routes. The routes in Sot De Chera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Sot De Chera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sot de Chera?
A: Sot de Chera offers a wide variety of hiking trails, with nearly 70 routes recorded on komoot. These trails cater to all fitness levels, ranging from easy paths to challenging routes, all set within the stunning Chera-Sot de Chera Natural and Geological Park.

Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes can I expect on hikes around Sot de Chera?
A: The terrain around Sot de Chera is incredibly diverse, characterized by dramatic deep gorges, towering mountains reaching up to 1,100 meters, and lush, fertile valleys. You'll encounter significant geological formations like travertines and impressive arch-shaped folds, as well as riparian forests along the River Sot. The area's rugged beauty is a defining feature of its hiking experience.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Sot de Chera?
A: Yes, many routes in Sot de Chera are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822491467/mirador-del-morroncillo-circular-from-sot-de-chera-pr-cv-223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Morroncillo — Circular from Sot de Chera (PR-CV 223)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views. Another excellent choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e823738011/la-cuesta-larga-pr-cv-414-circular-to-puntal-de-los-vagos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cuesta Larga (PR-CV 414) — Circular to Puntal de Los Vagos&lt;/a&gt;, providing a moderate challenge with varied scenery.

Q: Can I find hikes with waterfalls or natural pools in Sot de Chera?
A: Absolutely. The River Sot (Reatillo River) traverses the area, creating picturesque natural pools perfect for a refreshing dip, such as Charco Gruñidor. Many trails also lead to scenic waterfalls, including the notable Chorrero de la Castellana near the Cueva de la Garita. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e823774993/the-water-route-along-the-reatillo-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Route along the Reatillo River&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option specifically designed to showcase these aquatic features.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: Sot de Chera is rich in natural attractions. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Garita&lt;/a&gt;, discover the crystalline waters of Charco Azul, or visit the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buseo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is often part of various hiking routes. The area also features stunning geological formations like the graben of Chera and impressive anticlines.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Sot de Chera?
A: Yes, Sot de Chera offers several easy paths suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e823774993/the-water-route-along-the-reatillo-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Route along the Reatillo River&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, providing a gentle walk along the river with opportunities to enjoy natural pools. These trails are generally less demanding and allow for a relaxed exploration of the natural beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sot de Chera?
A: The best time to hike in Sot de Chera is generally during the spring and autumn months. The weather is milder, making for more comfortable hiking conditions. Spring brings lush greenery and blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful fall colors. Summer can be quite warm, but the natural pools provide a refreshing escape.

Q: How difficult are the hikes around Sot de Chera?
A: Hiking difficulty in Sot de Chera varies significantly. You'll find a range of options from easy, accessible paths suitable for casual strolls to challenging routes that demand good fitness or even alpine experience. Out of 71 total tours, 16 are easy, 43 are moderate, and 12 are difficult, ensuring there's a suitable trail for every ability level.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Sot de Chera?
A: The trails in Sot de Chera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse natural landscapes, the dramatic gorges, the presence of the Buseo Reservoir, and the picturesque Reatillo River. The well-developed network of approved PR and GR trails also contributes to a positive experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance or more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Sot de Chera offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e815805619/ruta-de-los-pantaneros-and-hanging-bridges-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Pantaneros and Hanging Bridges Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 20.7 km route featuring demanding terrain and incorporating unique hanging bridges. Another moderate option that covers a good distance is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e816949466/buseo-reservoir-and-barranco-de-la-hoz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buseo Reservoir and Barranco de la Hoz Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 14.7 km trail through varied landscapes.

Q: What makes the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural and Geological Park unique for hiking?
A: The Chera-Sot de Chera Natural and Geological Park is unique due to its profound geodiversity and biodiversity. It's home to the first geological park in the Region of Valencia, attracting visitors with spectacular rock formations like the graben of Chera and impressive anticlines. This geological richness, combined with its dramatic gorges, mountains, and lush valleys, creates a truly distinctive hiking environment.

Q: Are there cultural points of interest in Sot de Chera village to explore after a hike?
A: Yes, the village of Sot de Chera itself offers cultural points of interest. You can explore a historic castle atop a rock, visit the San Sebastián Mártir Parish Church, and see traditional structures like olive mills and a public washhouse. These add a cultural dimension to your nature-focused visit.
tips:
The Reatillo River, also known as the Sot River in its final stretch, is a tributary of the Turia River that flows through spectacular natural surroundings in the province of Valencia, particularly in the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park. In Sot de Chera, the river forms natural pools or "charcos" (pools), ideal for swimming, especially in summer.
One of the best known is Charco Gruñidor, a natural pool of crystal-clear water surrounded by vegetation and rocks.

The Reatillo River, also known as the Sot River in its final stretch, is a tributary of the Turia River that flows through spectacular natural surroundings in the province of Valencia, particularly in the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park. In Sot de Chera, the river forms natural pools or "charcos" (pools), ideal for swimming, especially in summer.
One of the best-known is Charco Gruñidor, a natural pool of crystal-clear water surrounded by vegetation and rocks.

faqs.question: How do I get to the trailhead for the Water Route along the Reatillo River?
faqs.answer: The Water Route is located within the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park. While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, the route is generally accessible, often starting from nearby villages like Chera or the hamlet of El Reatillo. Public transport options are limited, so driving is usually the most practical way to reach the area.

faqs.question: Is the Water Route along the Reatillo River dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs within the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park should be checked locally. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round, but the warmer months are ideal if you plan to take advantage of the natural pools for swimming. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for hiking, while summer can be hot but refreshing with the river access. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Water Route along the Reatillo River, as it is a public access trail within the natural park.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Water Route along the Reatillo River is generally considered an easy walk, making it suitable for most fitness levels, including families with children and beginners. The 4.17 km route with minimal elevation gain is very manageable. However, some sections might involve walking in the water or have slightly steeper parts, so good footwear is recommended.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Water Route?
faqs.answer: You will encounter a varied terrain, including unpaved and paved tracks, designated footpaths, and sometimes roads. The trail closely follows the Reatillo River, moving through Mediterranean forests, olive and almond groves, and dramatic ravines. Some parts might involve walking alongside rocky walls, and for the more adventurous, sections with cable guides and steps can be found.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary attraction is the Reatillo River itself, with its often crystalline waters forming numerous natural pools, or 'charcos'. A notable one is Charco Gruñidor, perfect for swimming. You'll also experience diverse landscapes, from agricultural fields to dense Mediterranean forests and impressive river gorges known as 'estrechos'.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for swimming on this trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The Water Route is famous for its natural pools, or 'charcos', along the Reatillo River. These are ideal for bathing, especially during the warmer months. Charco Gruñidor is one of the most well-known and picturesque spots for a refreshing dip.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The main route, which is about 4.17 km long, typically takes around 1 hour and 8 minutes to complete at a moderate pace. This duration doesn't include stops for swimming or enjoying the scenery, so plan for a longer outing if you wish to fully experience the natural pools.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other trails. You might find yourself on parts of the Sot de Chera - El Justal (84% covered), Camino de herradura Chera - Sot de Chera (57% covered), or PRCV 223 (52% covered) during your hike.
